name: Lagoons, vineyards and city tour of Laguardia
name_original: Lagunas, viñedos y city tour por Laguardia
region_intro: Hiking around Laguardia, located in the Rioja Alavesa wine region, offers a diverse landscape characterized by vineyards, lagoons, and notable peaks. The area features a mix of rolling hills and more challenging mountainous terrain, with elevations reaching over 1,400 meters. Trails often pass through agricultural areas and natural reserves, providing varied scenery for hikers. Best hiking trails around Laguardia The most popular hiking route is Lagoons, vineyards and city tour of Laguardia, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural exploration. Another top favourite among local hikers is Bodegas Ysios – Puerto del Toro loop from Laguardia, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, leading through varied terrain with views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Puerto del Toro – Cruz del Castillo (1,431 m) loop from San Bartolomé, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain to a peak, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Laguardia is defined by vineyards, lagoons, and prominent peaks, offering routes that range from easy strolls to challenging ascents. The network provides options for different ability levels, from leisurely walks to demanding mountain hikes. The routes in Laguardia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Laguardia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Laguardia?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes around Laguardia, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include easy strolls through vineyards, moderate paths around lagoons, and more challenging ascents to prominent peaks.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in Laguardia?
A: Yes, Laguardia offers several easy trails perfect for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3981042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Juan – Laguardia Old Town loop from Laguardia&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.7-mile (6.0 km) path that takes you through the historic town and surrounding areas, ideal for a leisurely walk.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Laguardia?
A: Hiking around Laguardia features a varied landscape. You'll encounter rolling hills covered in vineyards, serene lagoons, and more rugged mountainous terrain, with some routes leading to peaks over 1,400 meters. Trails often pass through agricultural areas and natural reserves.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Laguardia?
A: Yes, many routes in Laguardia are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051935510/lagoons-vineyards-and-city-tour-of-laguardia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoons, vineyards and city tour of Laguardia&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.1-mile (13.0 km) loop that combines natural beauty with cultural exploration.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while hiking?
A: Hikers can explore several interesting landmarks. The region is known for its mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2850737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Herrera&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5204913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Toro&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views. You can also discover fascinating caves such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3081770" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Kiliz Cave&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6168199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sala de los Moros (Moors' Cave)&lt;/a&gt;, which often have historical significance.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Laguardia?
A: While the guide doesn't specify a single 'best' time, the diverse landscape of vineyards and peaks suggests that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially during harvest season in the Rioja Alavesa wine region. Winter hikes might be possible, particularly on lower elevation trails, but higher peaks could experience colder conditions.

Q: Are there dog-friendly hiking trails in Laguardia?
A: Many trails in natural settings are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas like vineyards or near wildlife. Check local regulations for specific parks or reserves you plan to visit.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, Laguardia offers challenging routes for experienced hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5342542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Toro – Cruz del Castillo (1,431 m) loop from San Bartolomé&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 6.0-mile (9.7 km) trail with substantial elevation gain, leading to a prominent peak and offering rewarding views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Laguardia?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique blend of natural beauty, including vineyards and lagoons, with cultural exploration found in routes that pass through historic towns and offer views of prominent peaks.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Laguardia?
A: While specific public transport details for individual trailheads are not provided, Laguardia is a well-known town in the Rioja Alavesa region. It's recommended to check local bus schedules or regional transport options to see if they connect to the starting points of your chosen hikes.

Q: Are there any easy hikes with good parking options?
A: Many routes starting directly from Laguardia, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3981042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Juan – Laguardia Old Town loop from Laguardia&lt;/a&gt;, would likely have parking available within or near the town. For trails further afield, it's best to check the specific route details on komoot for parking information.

Q: Can I combine hiking with exploring historical sites?
A: Absolutely. Laguardia itself is a historic walled town. Many hikes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051935510/lagoons-vineyards-and-city-tour-of-laguardia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoons, vineyards and city tour of Laguardia&lt;/a&gt;, integrate exploration of the town with the surrounding natural landscape. You can also find historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5246381" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Cantabria Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; near some trails.
tips: This ecclesiastical fortress was built in the opposite part of the city in relation to the Basilica of Santa María de los Reyes, attached to the wall and completing the southeastern end of the fortified area of Laguardia. Its current appearance is the result of successive interventions, expansions and reforms dating back from the end of the 12th century to practically the middle of the 18th century.

The Prao de la Paul lagoon is one of the four wetlands in the Laguardia lagoon area. It is the only lagoon of the four of artificial origin. Although it has recently been drained to curb the spread of invasive species, it remains an important nesting point for birds.
The Carravalseca lagoon is the first of the Laguardia lagoon system that is found on the GR 38 leaving Oyón.
In my opinion the most beautiful of all the lagoons, although it also influences that it was seen a little from above.

Laguardia, the guard. You can really imagine that in this little town. In the evening the gates are closed and you are safe. Today, of course, lots of pintxo bars and nice places to relax. really worth a visit.
For a fee of 2€ you can enjoy a wonderful view around the land of the Rioja wine. Steep stairs, but not very difficult to master.
Imposing church on the north side of Laguardia.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa Maria de los Reyes
